user_query: Update fathers maple kissed taco option to Birria tacos

updated_paths: Root > MealPlan_Version 4 > Father > Day 7 > Dinner > DIY Taco Night — Maple-Kissed Skillet

update_results.path: Root > MealPlan_Version 4 > Father > Day 7 > Dinner > DIY Taco Night — Maple-Kissed Skillet

meal_suggestion.from: Assemble made‑to‑order tacos: warm corn tortillas, top with lean beef, shredded lettuce, diced tomato, cheddar, and salsa. Optional lime, avocado, or light sour cream. Pair with a simple side salad or beans for extra fiber.
meal_suggestion.to: Slow-cook beef (or lamb) with dried chiles, onion, garlic, and warming spices until tender. Shred the meat and dip corn tortillas into the rich braising consomé before pan-frying briefly to crisp edges. Fill tortillas with shredded birria meat, chopped onion, cilantro, and a squeeze of lime. Serve with small bowls of the consomé for dipping and extra lime wedges on the side.

nutrition_note.from: Lean beef provides high‑quality protein; corn tortillas supply carbohydrates; lettuce and tomato add volume and micronutrients; cheddar contributes calcium and fat; salsa offers flavor with relatively low calories. To boost fiber, use whole‑grain tortillas or add beans; to reduce sodium, opt for low‑sodium salsa and lighter cheese.
nutrition_note.to: Birria tacos are rich in protein from slow-cooked beef. The cooking method concentrates flavors and fat; draining or trimming excess fat and using modest portions of cheese or oil can reduce saturated fat. Serve with corn tortillas for a gluten-free base and add fresh cilantro and onion for fiber and micronutrients. Enjoy the consomé for added flavor but be mindful of sodium from braising liquids.

short_description.from: A quick, customizable taco night featuring lean beef, corn tortillas, fresh veggies, cheddar, and salsa.
